codeReview.summary: FileWriteTool atomic write implementation is correct. However, the implementation lacks path validation which is a critical security feature. WriteFileTool can write to any path on the filesystem, unlike ReadFileTool which properly validates paths are within workspace boundaries. This asymmetry is a security gap for an autonomous coding engine.

issues.description: WriteFileTool lacks workspace path validation. Compare to ReadFileTool (lines 18-54) which has: (1) workspace_path: Option<PathBuf> field, (2) with_workspace_path() builder method, (3) validate_path() method using canonicalize() to check path is within workspace. WriteFileTool has NONE of these. It can write to arbitrary paths like /etc/passwd or ~/.ssh/authorized_keys. This is a critical security vulnerability for an autonomous coding engine.
WriteFileTool struct definition is missing workspace_path field entirely. Without this field, there's no mechanism to configure or enforce workspace boundaries for file writes.

WriteFileTool::execute() accepts any path without validation. It creates temp file in path.parent() and writes there. No check ensures the path is within an allowed workspace. This allows writing to sensitive system locations.

test_write_file_tool only tests happy path - writes to temp dir and verifies content. Does not test: (1) overwriting existing file, (2) rollback when persist fails, (3) content size limit enforcement, (4) workspace boundary enforcement. No test exists for WriteFileTool similar to test_read_file_tool_workspace_validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description says 'Review the implementation in crates/swell-tools/src/file.rs' but actual implementation is in 'crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs' at line 147. This mismatch between task description and reality exists across file-read and file-edit reviews too.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Task prompt specifies 'file.rs' but WriteFileTool is at tools.rs:147

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker incorrectly concluded 'FileWriteTool with atomic writes and rollback was already fully implemented' and only fixed an unused-mut warning. The worker did not notice the missing path validation, which is inconsistent with ReadFileTool's implementation pattern. The handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true but the assessment was incomplete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff says 'already fully implemented' but lacks critical security feature. Compare ReadFileTool (lines 18-54) has validate_path but WriteFileTool does not.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services were started by the worker. The validation commands (cargo build, cargo test) are standard and documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows only build/test commands, no external services

summary: FileWriteTool FAILS review due to missing workspace path validation - a critical security gap. While the atomic write implementation (NamedTempFile + persist) is correct, WriteFileTool can write to any path on the filesystem. This contrasts with ReadFileTool which properly validates paths are within workspace using canonicalize(). The feature description does not explicitly require workspace validation, but the asymmetry with ReadFileTool and the security implications for an autonomous coding engine make this a blocking issue. Worker incorrectly assessed the implementation as 'fully implemented' when it lacks a core security feature established by the sibling ReadFileTool.
